title: Kazheesirama Vinnagaram
tagline: Kazheesirama Vinnagaram — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Kazheesirama Vinnagaram or Tadalan Kovil or Tiruvikrama (trivikara) Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Sirkazhi, of Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu
overview.description: Kazheesirama Vinnagaram or Tadalan Kovil or Tiruvikrama (trivikara) Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located in Sirkazhi, of Mayiladuthurai district, Tamil Nadu, India. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desam dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Trivikrama and his consort Lakshmi as Loganayagi. The temple is believed to have been built by Cholas, with later contributions from Medieval Ch

topPlaces.name: Kadamangudi
topPlaces.description: Kadamangudi is a village in Thanjavur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, situated along the river Kollidam. It consists of just over 100 families.

topPlaces.name: Palunkanda Nathar Temple
topPlaces.description: Palunkanda Nathar Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Thiruvaipadi near Thiruppanandal in the Thanjavur district of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Lower Anaicut
topPlaces.description: The Lower Anaicut or Anaikkarai is a dam and bridge built on the Kollidam (the northern distributary of Kaveri River) in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It was built in 1902 by the colonial administration of the Madras Presidency for irrigating the erstwhile South Arcot district.

topPlaces.name: Cholamadevi
topPlaces.description: Cholamadevi is a village in the Udayarpalayam Taluk of Ariyalur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Anaikarai
topPlaces.description: Anaikarai is a village panchayat under Thiruvidaimarudur Taluk in Tanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. Anaikarai connects with two major bridges.

topPlaces.name: Veenangeni
topPlaces.description: Veenangeni is a small village near Vadalur town, in Cuddalore district, in the state of Tamil Nadu, in India. Veenangeni is the gateway for entering into the village Mettukkuppam, where saint Vallalar Ramalinga Swamigal spent the end of his life.

topPlaces.name: Thiruppanandal
topPlaces.description: Thiruppanandal is a panchayat town in Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Kola Valvill Ramar Temple, Tiruvelliyangudi
topPlaces.description: Kolavalvil Ramar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu 19 km from Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu, India. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is mentioned in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Kola Valvill Ramar and his consort Lakshmi as Maragathavalli.

items.note: Kadamangudi is a village in Thanjavur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, situated along the river Kollidam. It consists of just over 100 families.
Palunkanda Nathar Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Thiruvaipadi near Thiruppanandal in the Thanjavur district of Tamil Nadu, India.

The Lower Anaicut or Anaikkarai is a dam and bridge built on the Kollidam (the northern distributary of Kaveri River) in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It was built in 1902 by the colonial administration of the Madras Presidency for irrigating the erstwhile South Arcot district.
Cholamadevi is a village in the Udayarpalayam Taluk of Ariyalur district, Tamil Nadu, India.
Anaikarai is a village panchayat under Thiruvidaimarudur Taluk in Tanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. Anaikarai connects with two major bridges.

Veenangeni is a small village near Vadalur town, in Cuddalore district, in the state of Tamil Nadu, in India. Veenangeni is the gateway for entering into the village Mettukkuppam, where saint Vallalar Ramalinga Swamigal spent the end of his life.
Thiruppanandal is a panchayat town in Thanjavur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

Kolavalvil Ramar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu 19 km from Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu, India. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is mentioned in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Kola Valvill Ramar and his consort Lakshmi as Maragathavalli.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kazheesirama Vinnagaram?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kazheesirama Vinnagaram is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kazheesirama Vinnagaram?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (87 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (88 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kazheesirama Vinnagaram?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kazheesirama Vinnagaram at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kazheesirama Vinnagaram?
faq.a: Stays in Kazheesirama Vinnagaram start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kazheesirama Vinnagaram?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kadamangudi, Palunkanda Nathar Temple, Lower Anaicut, Cholamadevi, Anaikarai.
